question: Which genres appear most often during periods of stylistic transition, as indicated by overlapping or mixed genre entries?

analysis.design_rationale: A heatmap visualizes the pairwise co-occurrence of genres within transitional artworks, revealing which genre combinations are most characteristic of stylistic transitions. This approach leverages a matrix format to enable exploration of not just individual genres, but their association patterns, providing insight into how genres interact and overlap during transitional periods.
analysis.target_insight: The visualization uncovers key genre pairs that define stylistic transitions, showing the strength and frequency of their co-occurrence among mixed genre entries.

transformations: Filter df to include only rows where df['genre'] contains signs of overlap (',' or '/').
Split genre strings into lists and enumerate all possible genre pairs per entry.
Aggregate pairwise co-occurrence counts across all transitional artworks.
Build a square matrix of genre vs. genre with counts as values.
data_preparation_steps: filtered['genre'] = filtered['genre'].apply(lambda x: [g.strip() for g in x])
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c2_sec\20.csv')
filtered = df[df['genre'].str.contains(',') | df['genre'].str.contains('/')].copy()
filtered['genre'] = filtered['genre'].str.replace('/', ',')
filtered = filtered.assign(genre=filtered['genre'].str.split(','))
filtered['genre'] = filtered['genre'].apply(lambda x: [g.strip() for g in x])
all_genres = sorted(set(sum(filtered['genre'].tolist(), [])))
pair_matrix = pd.DataFrame(0, index=all_genres, columns=all_genres)
for row in filtered['genre']:
    for i in range(len(row)):
        for j in range(len(row)):
            if i != j:
                pair_matrix.loc[row[i], row[j]] += 1
pair_matrix = pair_matrix.astype(int)
plt.figure(figsize=(11, 9))
cmap = plt.cm.Blues
im = plt.imshow(pair_matrix.values, cmap=cmap, vmin=0, vmax=pair_matrix.values.max())
plt.xticks(np.arange(len(all_genres)), all_genres, rotation=45, ha='right')
plt.yticks(np.arange(len(all_genres)), all_genres)
plt.title('Genre Co-occurrence Heatmap\nStylistic Transitions in Best Artworks of All Time')
plt.colorbar(im, fraction=0.045, pad=0.04)
for i in range(len(all_genres)):
    for j in range(len(all_genres)):
        val = pair_matrix.values[i, j]
        if val > 0:
            plt.text(j, i, str(val), ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, color='black')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
